mechanism_narrative: A higher federal funds rate directly increases the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, reducing the present value of equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This relationship is always active and does not require a specific threshold.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and forward returns is positive over a full cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.3 million units signals a deepening contraction in residential investment and household wealth, which depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1.3, positive forward 20-day returns occur in more than 60% of cases, or the signal is not significantly different from baseline (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve cuts the federal funds rate simultaneously with a rising unemployment rate, the tightening labor market offsets the discount rate benefit, and the market interprets the easing as reactive rather than preemptive, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In episodes where FEDFUNDS decreases and UNRATE increases concurrently over 3 months, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations, or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion phases, industrial production growth decelerates but remains positive, signaling a peak in economic activity and imminent recession, causing the S&P 500 to decline over the next 60 trading days as corporate earnings expectations deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 6-month growth rate > 0 and < 3%, and 3-month growth rate < 6-month growth rate
failure_condition: When INDPRO exhibits late-cycle deceleration as defined, forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of observations, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points in three months signals a weakening labor market that reduces disposable income and consumer sentiment, leading corporations to lower earnings guidance and the S&P 500 to fall over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 pp in 3 months, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than half of occurrences, or the average return is above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales reflects expanding consumer demand, supporting corporate revenue and earnings, and consistently leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between RSAFS and forward 20-day returns is negative or below 0.02 over any 2-year window, or the positive return incidence in high-RSAFS periods is not above random.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is declining while the federal funds rate remains elevated above 4%, the combination of contracting output and restrictive monetary policy amplifies recession fears and tight financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, forward 20-day returns are positive in at least 50% of instances, or the average return is not negative at the 10% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly acceleration in consumer price index exceeding 0.5% signals rising inflationary pressures that prompt expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: After a monthly CPI rise above 0.5%, forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the mean return is not significantly negative.
